title: Streaming Analysis, Chunk Boundaries, and Resource Budgets · Language Clinic: Stream Iteration
summary: A large-file mode still calls read_text, splits tokens at chunk edges, decodes partial UTF-8 incorrectly, retains every token, and claims bounded memory without measurement. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the stream iteration increment in bounded streaming analyzer. Learners progress through prediction, exp…
objectives: Process lines or bounded chunks incrementally and keep only state required by the declared report.
Label every metric with unit, inclusion policy, input version, and reconciliation or non-reconciliation rule.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Bookbot team handles case 6004 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove streaming and batch equivalence under token-fragment and peak-memory bounds; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Process lines or bounded chunks incrementally and keep only state required by the declared report. A defensible solution applies Stream Iteration, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to bounded streaming analyzer.

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-stream-iteration — Process lines or bounded chunks incrementally and keep only state required by the declared report.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-streaming-bounded-memory-3
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-streaming-bounded-memory-3.
# Case 5798: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def worked_bookbot_streaming_bounded_memory_3(chunks: int = 8, batch_tokens: int = 120, stream_tokens: int = 120, peak_bytes: int = 8192, memory_budget: int = 16384, fragments_closed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "policy3dekrow",
        "revision5798",
        "commandworked35798",
        "report3dekrow5798",
        "test5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if chunks < 1 or batch_tokens < 0 or stream_tokens < 0:
        return False, stream_tokens, "stream-shape"
    if stream_tokens != batch_tokens:
        return False, stream_tokens, "batch-stream-drift"
    if peak_bytes < 0 or peak_bytes > memory_budget:
        return False, peak_bytes, "memory-budget"
    if not fragments_closed:
        return False, chunks, "partial-token"
    assert peak_bytes <= memory_budget
    return True, stream_tokens, "bounded-stream"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Process lines or bounded chunks incrementally and keep only state required by the declared report.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Stream Iteration model · Theory Stream Iteration, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Stream Iteration precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Stream Iteration model · Theory Stream Iteration, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 5622 during a digital-humanities corpus revision. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove streaming and batch equivalence under token-fragment and peak-memory bounds; keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Process lines or bounded chunks incrementally and keep only state required by the declared report.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Stream Iteration model · Theory Stream Iteration, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Using a for loop over a file guarantees the whole pipeline has bounded memory.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-stream-iteration-ki3x8u.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-stream-iteration-ki3x8u(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_stream_iteration_ki3x8u\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?chunks < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?stream_tokens != batch_tokens)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?peak_bytes > memory_budget)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not fragments_closed)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
